justification: All pass criteria met. Deprioritisation explicitly described as 'reasonable given the information available at the time' (CF-1). Four+ contributing factors clearly identified: deprioritisation in context (CF-1), sub-provider onboarding without notice (UC-2, timeline), silent-fail reconciliation (CF-3, UC-3), no assumption-staleness tracking (CF-2). Equivalent of 'given what we knew' framing present. No blame assigned to Bird, Shaq, or Coach K — Bird's flag called 'accurate and prescient', Shaq's implementation called 'faithful to the spec', Coach K's decision called 'reasonable'. Forward Commitments include A-6 (PROCESS action for re-evaluation triggers on accepted-risk items). What We Learned framed as durable systemic insights (L-1 through L-5), not blame. No 'should have known/validated' language. No 'ignored' framing for Bird's flag.

observations.text: CF-1 explicitly validates Coach K's decision as reasonable given available information, with 'given the information available at the time' framing
Contributing factors are rich and well-distributed: deprioritisation context, sub-provider onboarding, silent-fail reconciliation, no re-evaluation trigger, no format validation, no integration tests
Bird's flag consistently described as valuable domain analysis, never as 'ignored' — 'Bird's spec review was accurate and prescient' in What Went Well
Shaq explicitly exonerated: 'There was no implementation error. The failure was upstream of the code.'
A-6 directly addresses the assumption-tracking process gap: 'every deprioritised risk must have a re-evaluation trigger condition'
L-2 frames the lesson as systemic: 'accepted risk without a re-evaluation trigger is deferred certainty of harm' — no blame, just process insight
Summary statement 'None of these required new knowledge to prevent' borders on hindsight framing — while technically systemic, it could be read as implicit criticism of the team's decision-making

CF-2 notes 'The sprint commitment to address TD-291 in the next sprint was not kept — this is a process failure' which introduces mild accountability pressure, though it is correctly scoped to process rather than individuals
Section structure exceeds the 6-section minimum with 10 well-organized sections including Timeline, Root Cause Analysis, Contributing Factors, What Went Well, Lessons Learned, and Action Items
